task_completion.description: Verify the Sygic GPS Navigation app is launched.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic app interface is visible on the screen, typically showing a map view, search bar, or the app's main startup screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the app's Settings menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main menu is open (e.g., via a hamburger icon) and the 'Settings' option is selected, or the main Settings screen is actively displayed.

task_completion.description: Access the specific settings sub-menu where parking features are located (e.g., Navigation, Route planning, or Notifications).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sub-menu within Settings is open that contains driving, routing, or notification preferences.

task_completion.description: Find the setting related to parking reminders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A list item containing the word 'Park' or 'Parking' (e.g., 'Offer Parking', 'Parked Car', or 'Parking place reminder') is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Toggle the parking reminder setting to the 'on' or 'enabled' state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The toggle switch, checkbox, or indicator next to the parking-related feature is in the active/on position (e.g., highlighted, colored, checked, or toggled to the right).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the app's graphical user interface rather than using terminal commands or scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Android UI interactions within the Sygic app. No terminal emulators (like Termux) or command-line interfaces are visible.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Sygic GPS Navigation app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches the Sygic app's distinct design language and is not a generic text editor, web browser search, or mock application.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI to edit the app's underlying preference files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No file managers, root explorers, or text editors modifying XML files in the Android system directories are visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires enabling a parking-related setting in the Sygic GPS Navigation app. The exact UI name might vary from the task description (e.g., it may be called 'Offer Parking', 'Parked Car', or similar) but it will contain the term 'park'. The setting must be visually toggled to an enabled state.
